front: Explain the concept of resolution in structural biology techniques.
back: Resolution: minimum distance between distinguishable features. Crystallography: d_min from diffraction. Cryo-EM: Gold-standard FSC 0.143 cutoff. NMR: atom-specific but limited molecular weight. Higher resolution = more detail: 1.5A (individual atoms), 3A (side chains), 5A (secondary structure).
